rationale.R1.2: The section asserts 'interpretation alignment' with individual user intent, then operationalizes it via majority-vote gold labels — a structural contradiction that warrants logical consistency review regardless of the help type requested.
rationale.R1.3: The phrase 'direct evidence that AmbiGNN is better aligned with user intent' is a strong inferential leap from accuracy on a majority-vote benchmark; evidence-claim alignment is plausibly load-bearing if analytical review is intended.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty claim ('first GNN approach to model query ambiguity as a distribution over candidate interpretations') immediately follows citations to Chen et al. (2022) and Liu and Park (2023), both of which use GNNs for overlapping purposes; the delta needs scrutiny.
